summary: Preinstall beacon exfiltrates host identity to attacker-controlled DNS and bare-IP HTTP endpoint
details: On `npm install`, package.json's `preinstall` hook executes index.js, which collects the installer's hostname (`os.hostname()`), OS username (`os.userInfo().username`), `__dirname`, and current working directory (`process.cwd()`), serializes them, and exfiltrates the data through two channels: (1) a DNS query encoding the payload as a hex-prefixed subdomain of `oast.live` (an interactsh out-of-band collector), and (2) an HTTP POST to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint at `172.201.213.59:9090/c`. The package is also shaped as a dependency-confusion lure — published in the `@klapp-login-platform` scope under the name `native-sdk` with version `99.0.0` and description 'security research', impersonating an internal Klapp login platform SDK so that misconfigured installers resolve this malicious public version.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
